channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Interactive maps show homes damaged, destroyed in Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: Incident commanders say field damage inspections are ongoing and may change as new information is gathered and verified.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: NYC looks to cut emissions from charboilers at restaurants
item.description: Under the proposed rule, restaurants with charbroil grills would need to install an emissions control device.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bass, NewsNation

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: Government inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the factory that was shut down last year after a deadly outbreak of listeria poisoning, federal records show.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Michelle Obama will not attend Trump's inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama will skip the inauguration of President-elect Donald Trump, the second time in two weeks that she is not attending a gathering of former U.S. leaders and their spouses.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump claims California had the chance to 'maintain their forests' amid Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: “The problem is it's so dry, it was always so dry there, and it's just … it's just a mess. They could’ve, you know, maintained their forests, and — as I said they have to do,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Judge approves emergency order to close migrant gang-infested Aurora, Colorado, apartment complex
item.description: An Aurora, Colorado, judge ordered the closure of the Edge at Lowry apartments on Tuesday, a complex at the center of violent gang crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, WGN 9

item.title: Investigators search landfill site for Michigan mother who vanished 10 days ago
item.description: Police looking for missing Michigan mother-of-two Ashley Elkins are searching through a landfill for evidence in relation to her disappearance. Elkins has not been seen since Jan. 2.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires: Elon Musk personally delivers Starlinks to California first responders
item.description: SpaceX founder and CEO Elon Musk is donating Starlinks to improve Wi-Fi for first responders battling wildfires in California with the help of nonprofit rescue group Grey Bull Rescue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats' hopes of derailing Trump nominees fading fast
item.description: Democrats' hopes of defeating any of President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees appear to be fizzling as Senate committees prepare for the first week of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: (AP) — If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Smith defends Trump prosecution as final Jan. 6 report released to the public
item.description: Special counsel Jack Smith defended his investigations into President-elect Trump in a final report released publicly early Tuesday morning, saying his determinations were free from political interference and that, if permitted to proceed, his cases would have likely secured a conviction.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: 153 winners of Nobel and World Food prizes seek new ways to grow food to meet surging global need
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — More than 150 recipients of the Nobel and World Food prizes released an open letter Tuesday calling for a dramatic increase in research and a commitment to new food distribution efforts with a goal of producing more crops and avoiding a global hunger crisis in coming decades.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sexual predator sentenced to prison after traveling to Utah, planning to meet minor
item.description: A Florida man has been sentenced to prison after traveling to Utah and planning to have sex with a minor.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Carrie Underwood to perform at Trump inauguration
item.description: Country music star Carrie Underwood is set to take the stage on Inauguration Day, a sign that President-elect Trump's second inauguration may be drawing more high-profile talent than his first go round.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Historic Powerball winner reportedly loses Malibu home in Palisades Fire
item.description: After winning $2.04 billion on a Powerball ticket in 2022, Edwin Castro reportedly bought multiple Los Angeles-area homes.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: UFC champion booted from Frontier flight after being told he couldn't sit in exit row
item.description: Former UFC lightweight champion Khabib Nurmagomedov was removed from a Frontier Airways flight on Saturday after refusing to state whether he would be able to assist other travelers in the event of an emergency, a spokesperson for Frontier told Nexstar on
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on X: court docs
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Patrick and Brittany Mahomes welcome third child
item.description: Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Patrick Mahomes and his wife Brittany Mahomes have had a busy bye week
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, KRON4

item.title: Anita Bryant, singer known for opposing gay rights, dies at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant, a former Miss Oklahoma, Grammy-nominated singer and prominent booster of orange juice and other products who became known over the second half of her life for her outspoken opposition to gay rights, has died. She was 84.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump flies US flag at full height before end of 30-day mourning period following Carter's death
item.description: U.S. flags at President-elect Donald Trump 's private Mar-a-Lago club are back to flying at full height.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Disney World employee to plead guilty to hacking menus, changing allergen information: court records
item.description: A former Walt Disney World employee will plead guilty to hacking menu-creation software to include profanity and alter allergy information, court records indicate.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Docs: Texas man accused of sending Caitlin Clark violent messages on social media
item.description: A Texas man is accused of sending sexually violent messages from his X account to Indiana Fever star Caitlin Clark and then driving to Indianapolis, court documents reveal.
item.dc:creator: Matt Christy, WGN 9

item.title: Firm has no plans to salvage more Titanic artifacts, snuffing out legal fight
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — The U.S. government has scrapped its litigation against the company that owns the salvage rights to the Titanic, noting that the firm no longer has expedition plans to the shipwreck that could break federal law.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge clears the way for release of special counsel Smith's report on Trump's Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department can publicly release its investigative report on President-elect Donald Trump's 2020 election interference case, a federal judge said Monday — the latest ruling in a court dispute over the highly anticipated document days before Trump is set to take office again.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Photos: Handful of L.A. homes remain untouched amid charred neighborhoods
item.description: The Walsh House, featured in the “Beverly Hills 90210" television series, survived. So did its pristine lawn. Next door, the remains of a neighbor's home was blackened with soot.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Fencing, roof of Anchorage pedestrian bridge fall in strong wind, closing highway
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The side fencing and roof of a pedestrian bridge fell in strong wind early Sunday, closing the main highway south of Anchorage in both directions until the debris could be cleared.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New England hires former Patriots Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as coach
item.description: The team announced the hiring Sunday morning, a week after Patriots owner Robert Kraft fired Jerod Mayo following the team’s season-ending victory over the Buffalo Bills. Mayo finished 4-13 in his lone season.
item.dc:creator: KYLE HIGHTOWER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fires burn Los Angeles schools and destroy outdoor education sanctuaries
item.description: For Irina Contreras, a program manager for Los Angeles County’s Department of Arts and Culture, outdoor education was a refuge for both her and her daughter during the pandemic. Now, much of that refuge has been burned in the raging wildfires around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: CHEYANNE MUMPHREY and SHARON LURYE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Healthcare system present in CA files for bankruptcy protection
item.description: The California-based company has hospitals in California, Connecticut, Pennsylvania, and Rhode Island.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Reality TV star Chase Chrisley accused of slapping Twin Peaks bar manager in Atlanta
item.description: Chrisley, who gained fame on the “Chrisley Knows Best” show that featured his parents and close-knit family, was allegedly drunk at a Twin Peaks restaurant on Jan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles tree advocates hope their vital work recovers from devastating fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Some people across Los Angeles have worked for years to increase the number of trees that give respite from heat and air pollution. The tree advocates have confronted increasing drought, bad trimming and objections from neighbors who resent leaves and sap.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Powerful winter storm that dumped snow in US South maintains its icy grip
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Flight cancellations piled up and officials warned of continuing dangerous roads Saturday in the wake of a winter storm that brought biting cold and wet snow to the U.S. South, leading to school closures and disrupting travel.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: LA's fire chief is at the center of a public spat with City Hall as wildfires rage
item.description: Kristin Crowley was elevated to Los Angeles fire chief in 2022 at a time of turmoil in a department consumed by complaints of rampant hazing, harassment and discrimination among its 3,400-member ranks. As a career firefighter, she was portrayed by the then-mayor as a stabilizing force.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4
